reasoning: Founding member of the Medicare for All Caucus and original cosponsor of the Medicare for All Act in every Congress since its inception (H.R. 1384 in the 116th, H.R. 1976 in the 117th, H.R. 3421 in the 118th, H.R. 3069 in the 119th). Has stated 'Healthcare is a human right'  and called for making Medicare available to all Americans.

Lead sponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act since 2013, which would create a federal right to abortion free from medically unnecessary restrictions.  Also cosponsors the EACH Act to repeal the Hyde Amendment and enable federal funding for abortion care.  Has called abortion 'a fundamental human right.'

Applauded the Supreme Court's 2025 decision striking down Trump's tariffs, calling them 'reckless' and 'a massive tax increase on American families and small businesses.' As a Ways and Means Committee member  with trade jurisdiction,  she supports targeted trade sanctions (e.g. on Russia) but opposes broad protectionist tariffs.

Called the 2025 GOP tax bill a 'tax scam' that 'prioritizes the ultra-wealthy at the expense of working families.'  Introduced the End Tax Breaks for Dark Money Act, led efforts demanding IRS funding to 'pursue wealthy tax cheats,'  and cosponsored the Social Security Expansion Act requiring millionaires and billionaires to pay their fair share by lifting the income cap.

Cosponsored the Respect for Marriage Act (signed into law 2022) repealing DOMA and requiring states to recognize same-sex marriages. Introduced the PRIDE Act (H.R. 4326, 2023; reintroduced 2025) to update tax code language to be gender-neutral and retroactively provide tax refunds to same-sex couples.  Serves as Vice Chair of the House Equality Caucus.

Original cosponsor of the Equality Act, which amends the Civil Rights Act to prohibit discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity  and explicitly limits the Religious Freedom Restoration Act from overriding its anti-discrimination protections.  Stated discrimination against LGBTQ individuals 'is completely unjustifiable and cannot be tolerated.'

Cosponsored the Equality Act which prohibits discrimination based on gender identity in education including sports. Explicitly stated 'Discrimination against LGBTQ individuals in where they can live, work, or eat, how they must go to the bathroom, what sports they can play and more is completely unjustifiable.'  Also cosponsored the Trans Bill of Rights (2026).

Voted for Ukraine aid (H.R. 8035) on April 20, 2024, stating 'Ukraine can finally receive economic and civilian security assistance and access to the munitions and weapons it has desperately needed.'  Has consistently voted to support Ukraine and send along humanitarian and military assistance,  and supported trade sanctions on Russia.

Founding member of the Medicare for All Caucus and original cosponsor of the Medicare for All Act in every Congress. Opposes Republican efforts to 'cut or privatize Medicare by automatically enrolling seniors in Medicare Advantage.'  Supports expanding Medicare to cover vision, hearing, and dental,  and hosted a 'Medicaid Day of Action Town Hall' in 2025 opposing proposed cuts.

States 'I am working to end our reliance on fossil fuels.'  Co-introduced the Polluters Pay Climate Fund Act (2024, reintroduced 2025) requiring fossil fuel companies to pay $100 billion/year into a climate fund.  Voted against the GOP's Lower Energy Costs Act which would expand drilling on public lands. Member of the Congressional Sustainable Energy and Environment Coalition.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes automatic voter registration, same-day registration, expansion of early voting and mail-in voting,  and prohibits voter roll purges.  Cosponsored the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act (H.R. 14 in 119th Congress).  Stated H.R. 1 would 'ensure that every eligible American has the chance for their voice to be heard.'

Introduced the Southeast Asian Deportation Relief Act (2023, 2026)  to halt deportations of refugees and create pathways for those already deported to return.  Introduced the POWER Act protecting immigrant workers from deportation threats.  Supports the U.S. Citizenship Act creating a path to citizenship for 11 million undocumented immigrants  but does not advocate stopping all deportations entirely.

Cosponsored both the Social Security Fairness Act  (signed into law Jan 2025, repealing WEP/GPO)  and the Social Security Expansion Act, which would expand benefits by $2,400/year and extend solvency through the century by removing the income cap so 'millionaires and billionaires pay their fair share.'  Opposes any cuts or privatization.

Cosponsored the AI Civil Rights Act (reintroduced Dec 2025) which imposes strict guardrails on AI, requires algorithms to be tested before and after deployment, mandates transparency, and prevents biased/discriminatory AI decisions.  Also drafting legislation mandating transparency in how Medicare Advantage plans use AI.  Her approach favors close government monitoring and enforced standards.

Declares 'I am committed to taking aggressive action to halt and reverse the effects of climate change.'  Supports ending reliance on fossil fuels, co-introduced the Polluters Pay Climate Fund Act,  cosponsored carbon pricing legislation,  voted for the Inflation Reduction Act's climate investments. Member of the Congressional Sustainable Energy and Environment Coalition supporting 'measures to phase out fossil fuels.'

Strongly supports strengthening civil rights enforcement and addressing systemic discrimination. Chaired the Congressional Asian Pacific American Caucus,  led DEI efforts with Fortune 100 companies, cosponsored the Equality Act  and the John Lewis Voting Rights Act, supported hate crime legislation, and participated in the 'Stop the Blame' campaign against anti-Asian rhetoric.

Secured $4 million for affordable housing units in Claremont  and $17.9 million total in community project funding for her district. Voted for Build Back Better which invested in construction of over 1 million affordable homes and improved the Low-Income Housing Tax Credit.  Introduced the Mortgage Relief for Disaster Survivors Act (H.R. 2928) in 2025.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes requiring disclosure of large donors, creating a system for matching individual campaign contributions, and strengthening oversight of elections.  Introduced the End Tax Breaks for Dark Money Act (2024) to close loopholes allowing wealthy donors to funnel tax-free money to political dark money groups.

Supports comprehensive immigration reform with a path to citizenship for 11 million undocumented immigrants. Introduced the Reuniting Families Act to reduce family visa backlogs,  sponsored SEADRA protecting Southeast Asian refugees,  cosponsored the American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 1589, 2025) for Dreamers. Opposes the Trump Muslim ban through her NO BAN Act.

Cosponsored the Stop Anti-Abortion Disinformation Act to crack down on crisis pregnancy centers spreading disinformation.  Cosponsored the AI Civil Rights Act requiring algorithmic transparency and testing.  Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes transparency requirements for political advertising.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which would 'eliminate gerrymandering'  by requiring independent redistricting commissions for congressional districts. Explicitly cited this provision as key to restoring 'fair representation in our government.' California already uses an independent citizen redistricting commission which she supports.

Original cosponsor of the Medicare for All Act in both the 116th Congress (H.R. 1384, 2019) and 117th Congress (H.R. 1976, 2021). Co-introduced the Health Care Emergency Guarantee Act with Bernie Sanders in 2020 to have Medicare pay all medical expenses for uninsured Americans.  Her congressional website states healthcare should be 'a right, not a privilege.'

Earned a 100% rating from both NARAL Pro-Choice America and Planned Parenthood Action Fund  every year during her congressional tenure.  Voted for the Women's Health Protection Act and the Ensuring Women's Right to Reproductive Freedom Act in 2022. Voted against every abortion restriction bill including the No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act and the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act.

As LA mayor in 2025, convened Port of LA roundtables criticizing Trump tariffs' 'unpredictability' and economic disruption, noting LA's port handles the most cargo in the Western Hemisphere.  In Congress, sponsored the Strengthening the AfCFTA Act of 2022 promoting African continental free trade.  Generally supports trade engagement and opposed protectionist tariff policies.

Voted against the 2017 GOP Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, calling it 'massive tax breaks to major corporations and the wealthiest Americans.'  Rated only 14% by the National Taxpayers Union (indicating support for higher taxes).  As mayor, supported Measure A, a sales tax increase to fund homelessness services.  Consistently supports taxing the wealthy and corporations.

Voted for the Respect for Marriage Act (H.R. 8404) in 2022 to codify marriage equality federally, stating 'all Americans have had the right to marry the person whom they love.'  Voted for the Equality Act in 2019. Named 2018 LA Stonewall Democratic Club Public Official of the Year.  Co-sponsored the LGBTQ+ Museum Act.

Voted for the Equality Act (2019, 2021), which bans LGBTQ discrimination with tight restrictions on religious liberty exemptions. Also voted for the Respect for Marriage Act, which included some religious liberty protections for nonprofits. Her record shows she protects religious freedom but does not allow it to override anti-discrimination protections.

Cosponsored the Transgender Bill of Rights resolution (H.Res. 1209, 2022) calling for federal protection of transgender rights.  Voted for the Equality Act which bans discrimination based on gender identity in education including school sports under Title IX. Visited The TransLatin@ Coalition offices as mayor  and recognized Transgender Day of Remembrance in Congress.

Voted for the $40 billion Additional Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act (H.R. 7691, 2022) and the Continuing Appropriations and Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act. Cosponsored the Countering Malign Russian Activities Globally Act (H.R. 9374, 2022).  Consistently supported Ukraine military and humanitarian aid at levels proposed by the Biden administration.

Original cosponsor of the Medicare for All Act in both 2019 (H.R. 1384) and 2021 (H.R. 1976), which would establish a national single-payer health insurance program. Voted over 50 times against Republican attempts to repeal or undermine the ACA.  Co-introduced the Health Care Emergency Guarantee Act with Sanders to have Medicare cover all uninsured Americans during COVID.

As LA mayor, pursuing 100% clean energy by 2035,  announced full coal divestment in December 2025,  and created a climate cabinet.  In Congress, co-sponsored H.Con.Res.119 calling for a climate recovery plan to 'phase out fossil fuel emissions.' The League of Conservation Voters gave her consistently high scores. Her approach is a rapid but not immediate phase-out.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act, 2019 and 2021), which mandates automatic voter registration, same-day registration, expanded mail-in and early voting, and independent redistricting commissions.  Also voted for H.R. 5746 (Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act). These bills represent the most expansive voting rights proposals in recent legislative history.

As mayor, signed multiple executive directives (ED 12 in July 2025,  ED 17 in August 2025) reinforcing LA's sanctuary city status, banning city cooperation with ICE, and proposing cash aid to families affected by immigration raids.  Sued the Trump administration over 'unconstitutional, reckless raids.'  In Congress, voted against deportation enforcement bills and supported DACA protections.

Stated 'Social Security and Medicare are promises that the federal government needs to keep' and has 'unequivocally opposed any attempt to slash funding.' Earned a 100% rating from the Alliance for Retired Americans.  Her record centers on protecting and strengthening existing benefits and opposing cuts rather than the most expansive expansion proposals like removing the income cap.

As mayor, pursuing 100% clean energy for LA by 2035,  divested from coal in 2025,  created a climate cabinet, and signed international climate cooperation agreements.  In Congress, cosponsored the Climate Action Now Act (H.R. 9, 2019). Called climate change 'an urgent crisis' and said 'Los Angeles needs to be the tip of the spear.'

Authored the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act (lead House sponsor in 2020 and 2021), the most comprehensive police reform bill ever passed by the House,  banning chokeholds and restricting qualified immunity.  Chaired the Congressional Black Caucus  (2019-2021).  As mayor, created the Office of Community Safety.  Voteview ranks her more liberal than 97% of the House.

As her first act as mayor, declared a state of emergency on homelessness  and committed $1.3 billion—a record city investment. Launched Inside Safe to move people from encampments into housing, signed Executive Directive 1 to fast-track affordable housing  (18,000+ units proposed), and backed Measure A (sales tax increase for homelessness).

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act), which includes the DISCLOSE Act to trace dark money donations,  small-dollar donation matching, and comprehensive campaign finance reform. Signed a letter advocating for 'comprehensive legislation to create a more accountable and transparent campaign finance system.'

Voted for the American Dream and Promise Act (H.R. 6) providing a path to citizenship for Dreamers and TPS holders.  Co-sponsored comprehensive immigration reform with a path to citizenship for 11 million undocumented immigrants. As mayor, signed executive directives reinforcing sanctuary city policies  and sued the Trump administration over ICE raids.  Rated 0% by FAIR.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes the DISCLOSE Act and transparency provisions for digital political advertising. Supported requiring disclosure of who is spending money on political ads online. Her general alignment supports mandating transparency and fact-checking rather than banning government involvement.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) in both the 116th and 117th Congresses, which mandates independent citizen redistricting commissions in every state for congressional maps. Notably, as California Assembly Speaker (2008-2010)  she opposed the California Citizens Redistricting Commission,  but her subsequent congressional votes for H.R. 1 (2019, 2021) represent an evolution to supporting independent commissions.

Member of the Medicare for All Caucus and original cosponsor of the Medicare for All Act in every Congress since entering office—H.R. 676 (2017), H.R. 1976 (2021), and H.R. 3421 (2023).  In 2026, held a Day of Action roundtable opposing Medicaid cuts,  stating 'Americans pay too much and receive too little.'

Holds a 100% score from Reproductive Freedom for All (2024)  and an F from SBA Pro-Life America. Voted for the Women's Health Protection Act (2021, 2022) which would create unlimited federal abortion rights,  voted against the Born-Alive Survivors Protection Act (Jan 2023),  and called abortion 'a fundamental right.'

Cosponsored H.R. 7615 (119th Congress, Feb 2026) to require refund of tariffs collected under IEEPA.  Introduced the 'Stop the Trump Electricity Price Hikes Act' (H.R. 5673, 2025) opposing tariff-driven energy cost increases.  Her district includes the Port of Los Angeles  and she has consistently opposed Trump's broad tariff policies.

Advocates for closing tax loopholes for fossil fuel companies and ensuring the wealthy 'pay their fair share.' Co-introduced the End Polluter Welfare Act (2020) to eliminate $15 billion in fossil fuel subsidies.  Cosponsored the Social Security 2100 Act which raises the payroll tax income cap.  As a Congressional Progressive Caucus member, consistently supports significantly raising taxes on wealthy individuals and corporations.

Voted YES on the Respect for Marriage Act both in July 2022 and again in December 2022,  stating 'Everyone deserves the right to live freely, love who they love, and marry the person they want to marry.'  Endorsed by Equality California and Stonewall Democratic Club, and called for full federal protections.

Cosponsored the Do No Harm Act in both the 115th Congress (H.R. 3222, 2017) and 117th Congress (H.R. 1378, 2021),  which limits religious exemptions under RFRA when they would harm third parties, override anti-discrimination laws, or deny benefits and services. This positions her as protecting religious freedom while preventing it from overriding civil rights protections.

Named a 'Trans Equality Champion' by Advocates for Trans Equality for the 118th Congress. Opposed H.R. 734 (Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act) which banned transgender girls from school sports.  Cosponsored the Equality Act (H.R. 5) and H.Res. 1058 (Transgender Bill of Rights, Feb 2026).  Member of the Congressional Equality Caucus.

Voted for H.R. 6833, the Continuing Appropriations and Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act (2022), providing Ukraine aid.  Supported the $95.3 billion security supplemental in 2024 but objected to tying Ukraine aid to restrictive border provisions.  Did not push for dramatically increased aid beyond what was proposed.

Member of the Medicare for All Caucus and cosponsor of the Medicare for All Act in the 115th, 117th, and 118th Congresses (most recently H.R. 3421 in May 2023).  Fought against proposed Medicaid cuts, stating 'Medicaid is a lifeline for more than 10.4 million Californians' in a Feb 2026 press conference.

Co-authored the Future Generations Protection Act (introduced 2020, 2021, 2023, and 2025) which would ban greenhouse gas emissions from all new power plants, stop hydraulic fracking, and ban crude oil and natural gas exports. Co-introduced the End Polluter Welfare Act to eliminate $15 billion in fossil fuel subsidies. Stated 'increasing our dependence on fossil fuels is incompatible with a habitable planet.'

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act, 2019 and 2021) which includes automatic voter registration, same-day registration, online voter registration, expanded early/mail-in voting, and restoring voting rights for felons. Also voted for H.R. 5746 (Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act). Worked on Obama's 2012 voter protection team in Florida.

As Chair of the Congressional Hispanic Caucus (2023-2024), strongly defended DACA and fought against mass deportations, stating 'seeing the deportation of hard-working and peaceful families hits home for me.' Cosponsored the American Dream and Promise Act providing pathway to citizenship for Dreamers. Her supported legislation still includes criminal background scrutiny for immigration applications.

Co-introduced the Social Security 2100 Act (H.R. 1902, 2017) which would expand benefits, provide a benefit bump for current and future beneficiaries, raise the threshold on taxation of benefits, adopt the CPI-E formula for COLA adjustments, and raise the income cap. Stated Social Security programs should be 'strengthened and expanded.'

Introduced the bipartisan AI Public Awareness and Education Campaign Act (H.R. 7151, Jan 2026) and the HEAL AI Act (Nov 2025) for medical AI literacy. Cosponsored the Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2023 requiring accountability assessments for AI systems and the Securing Elections from AI Deception Act. Her approach focuses on transparency and basic safety testing rather than heavy-handed bans.

Co-authored the Future Generations Protection Act calling for a 'rapid shift away from fossil fuels to clean renewable energy,' banning fracking and new fossil fuel infrastructure. Supported the CLEAN Future Act's target of 100% clean electricity by 2035 with an 80% mid-range target by 2030. Introduced climate justice grants legislation ($1B/year) and the Climate Smart Ports Act.

Cosponsored the Equity and Inclusion Enforcement Act and the Equality Act (H.R. 5). As CHC Chair, led the Tri-Caucus effort to restore the House Office of Diversity and Inclusion after Republicans defunded it. Has championed addressing the 'school-to-prison pipeline' as a civil rights issue and worked with the NAACP on health disparities.

Co-chairs the Caucus on Homelessness. Introduced the Yes in God's Backyard Act (H.R. 7152, Jan 2026) to empower faith-based institutions to build affordable housing. Reintroduced the Returning Home Act (H.R. 7057, Jan 2026) for housing formerly incarcerated people. Secured millions in federal affordable housing funding and stated she is 'dedicated to ensuring that everyone in my district has access to affordable housing.'

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act, 2019 and 2021) which includes public financing of congressional campaigns through small-dollar donation matching (6:1 match), requires additional disclosure of campaign donors, mandates disclaimers on political advertising, and expands prohibitions on campaign spending by foreign nationals.

As Chair of the Congressional Hispanic Caucus, champions comprehensive immigration reform with pathways to citizenship. Cosponsored the American Dream and Promise Act, strongly defended DACA, and pushed for the American Hope Act. Told NPR regarding asylum restrictions 'We are saying that we're going to put basically an end to asylum. We cannot allow that to happen.'

Cosponsored the Securing Elections from AI Deception Act (2024) which would prohibit use of AI to defraud voters and require disclaimers on AI-generated content, enforced by the FTC. Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes provisions prohibiting deceptive practices in elections and requiring transparency in political advertising.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act, 2019 and 2021) which requires all states to establish independent citizen redistricting commissions for congressional redistricting, banning partisan gerrymandering. Also voted for H.R. 5746 (Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act) which contains similar redistricting reform provisions.

Strongly supports the ACA and expanding coverage but has NOT endorsed Medicare for All. The Courage California Progressive Voters Guide notes she 'has not publicly supported Medicare for All and accepts significant donations from health industry PACs.' Member of the centrist New Democrat Coalition. In 2019 said she wanted 'an open debate about the feasibility' of Medicare for All while focusing on ACA improvements.

Member of the Pro-Choice Caucus and voted for the Women's Health Protection Act. In Jan 2026 introduced H.J.Res. 144, a CRA resolution to block Trump's VA abortion rule rollback. Introduced the Reproductive Freedom for Veterans Act in 2025. Brought a Planned Parenthood clinician as her 2024 SOTU guest. Called anti-choice GOP bills 'about control.'

As a member of the New Democrat Coalition's Trade and Tariffs Task Force, joined 44 colleagues in Feb 2026 demanding the Trump administration refund $175 billion in 'illegal tariffs' following the Supreme Court's ruling. Stated Trump's tariffs 'raised the cost of groceries, household goods, housing, and everyday life for working Americans.'

Voted against the 2017 GOP Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, calling it 'a windfall for the wealthy and corporations enjoying historic profits' that 'robbed the middle class.' Voted for the Build Back Better Act (2021) stating it was 'fully paid for by making big corporations and the wealthiest pay their fair share.' Supports closing corporate tax loopholes but focuses on middle-class tax relief rather than the most expansive tax increases.

Voted for the Respect for Marriage Act (Dec 2022). Introduced the Amend the Code for Marriage Equality Act multiple times (2019, 2022, 2025) to replace all gendered terms 'husband' and 'wife' in federal law with 'spouse.' Participated in the NOH8 Campaign in 2013. Endorsed by Equality California.

Cosponsored and voted for the Equality Act (H.R. 5, 117th Congress), which explicitly limits RFRA from being used as a defense against LGBTQ anti-discrimination protections. Named a 'Trans Equality Champion' by Advocates for Trans Equality. Supported the Respect for Marriage Act which includes some religious liberty protections but primarily ensures marriage equality.

Has not taken a specific on-the-record position directly addressing transgender athletes, but her broader legislative record shows strong support for transgender inclusion. Cosponsored the Transgender Bill of Rights (H.Res. 269, 2024 and 2026 reintroduction). Voted for the Equality Act protecting transgender students in education. Voted against the FY2024 NDAA over its anti-LGBTQ provisions. Supported VA gender-affirming surgery.

Voted for HR 5692 (Ukraine Security Assistance and Oversight Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2024), HR 6968 (Suspending Energy Imports from Russia Act), and HR 7108 (Suspending Normal Trade Relations with Russia and Belarus Act). Also supported the REPO for Ukrainians Act (H.R. 4175) to confiscate Russian sovereign assets for Ukraine reconstruction.

Rated 100% by both the Alliance for Retired Americans and the National Committee to Preserve Social Security and Medicare. Does NOT support Medicare for All (per Courage California) but fiercely defends and wants to expand current Medicare and Medicaid. In Feb 2026 denounced proposed Republican Medicaid cuts of 'nearly $1 trillion.' Voted for the Inflation Reduction Act which allowed Medicare to negotiate drug prices.

Served on the House Select Committee on the Climate Crisis. Opposed Trump's plan to allow new oil and gas drilling off the California Coast. Introduced the Green Energy for Federal Buildings Act requiring 100% renewable by 2050 and the Green Federal Fleet Act requiring zero-emission federal vehicles. Does NOT support the Green New Deal. Her position is to stop new fossil fuel permits while transitioning to clean energy.

Authored the Same Day Registration Act (H.R. 93) requiring all states to allow same-day voter registration for federal elections. Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes expanded early and mail-in voting, automatic voter registration, and prohibitions on voter roll purges. Co-authored legislation to end partisan gerrymandering.

In Jan 2026 held a press conference condemning ICE's 'brutality and lawlessness,' demanding 'an end to the detention and deportation of U.S. citizens, asylum seekers, refugees, and immigrants without due process.' Supports the DREAM Act for path to citizenship. Voted NO on granting ICE billions without oversight. Her framing is about due process and targeting violent offenders, not stopping all deportations.

Rated 100% by both the Alliance for Retired Americans and the National Committee to Preserve Social Security and Medicare. In March 2025 held a press conference denouncing Trump/DOGE attacks on Social Security, saying 'more than 70 million Americans rely on Social Security' and 'without Social Security, one in three Americans would be pushed into poverty.' Introduced bills to protect SSA from government shutdowns.

Served on the House Select Committee on the Climate Crisis and contributed to the 2020 Climate Crisis Action Plan. Supports 'strategic investments in clean, renewable, and sustainable energy' but does NOT support the Green New Deal. Her Green Energy for Federal Buildings Act targets 100% renewable by 2050 (not 2030). Voted for the Inflation Reduction Act's climate provisions. Opposes new offshore drilling.

Voted for the Equality Act (H.R. 5, 117th Congress) to expand civil rights protections to LGBTQ people. Named a 'Trans Equality Champion' by Advocates for Trans Equality for the 118th Congress. Cosponsored the Transgender Bill of Rights. States it is her 'duty to honor, respect, and protect the Constitutional rights of all Americans.'

Extensively worked on housing issues particularly for veterans. Introduced the Homeless Veteran Families Act (H.R. 4099) and secured millions in HUD funding for Ventura County affordable housing programs. Introduced the Mortgage Insurance Tax Deduction Act and Mortgage Debt Forgiveness Act. Voted for Build Back Better which included significant affordable housing investments.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) described as legislation to 'fight special interests, take money out of politics, and finally put the power of our democracy back in the hands of the people.' In 2014 joined colleagues supporting a constitutional amendment to 'allow Congress and the states to set reasonable limits on money in politics' to overturn Citizens United.

Supports the DREAM Act and DACA. Invited a DREAMer as her guest to the 2017 SOTU. Supports comprehensive immigration reform. In 2026 condemned ICE raids, demanded end to warrantless arrests and raids on churches/schools, and called for 'humane conditions in detention centers.' Advocates significantly increasing legal pathways and providing paths to citizenship while opposing mass enforcement.

Voted for H.R. 1 (For the People Act) which includes extensive disclosure requirements for political advertising—including digital ads—to 'shine a light on dark money' and requires organizations involved in political activity to disclose donors. Supports transparency and disclosure requirements for campaign-related content.

Co-authored legislation with Reps. Lofgren and Lowenthal specifically requiring all states to adopt independent citizen redistricting commissions modeled on California's system. This bill was included in H.R. 1 (For the People Act). Explicitly stated this legislation would 'end partisan gerrymandering' and make 'politicians more accountable to the voters.' This is one of her signature democracy reform issues.
